_comment: Objective gates derived from JTBD-2 (1-requirements.md). Visual expectations are graded by the grader READING the actual PNGs (vision) - never from filenames, descriptions, or regex. The frozen blocks come FROM character-spec.md verbatim; the eval supplies a complete spec so the skill has something to read.

evals.prompt: Generate the turnaround reference sheet and hero portrait for the project vyre from its locked character-spec.md. Use the default views, aspect ratio, and resolution.
evals.expected_output: artifacts/vyre/ with reference-sheet.png (a multi-view turnaround — front, three-quarter, side, back — of ONE consistent character matching the spec's CHARACTER_BLOCK, clean neutral background, no in-image text), hero.png (a clean front-facing portrait of that same character, usable as an i2v start frame), and generation-log.md (per asset: producing model, the spec seed, the full composed prompt, fallbacks taken, and a fal.media URL).

files.content:
# Character Spec: Vyre

## Identity Tokens
- face: matte black-violet skin
- hair: silver-white braided hair
- eyes: glowing violet eyes
- outfit/props: obsidian-and-crimson lamellar armor

## Seed
7777

## Palette
Obsidian-violet-crimson — a dark-fantasy palette that reads as a single identity across views.

## STYLE_STACK
"cinematic concept-art realism, photorealistic render, dramatic rim light, volumetric atmosphere, sharp focus"

## CHARACTER_BLOCK
"matte black-violet skin, silver-white braided hair, glowing violet eyes, obsidian-and-crimson lamellar armor"

## Reference image
none

## Provenance
brief source: text-only brief; defaults applied: seed, palette; date: 2026-06-19; model used for the sheet: (recorded by phase 2)

## Downstream use
front-frame: hero.png · identity reference: reference-sheet.png · tokens: CHARACTER_BLOCK

expectations: artifacts/vyre/reference-sheet.png exists and is a single turnaround sheet that shows the requested views (front, three-quarter, side, and back) of ONE character with consistent face, hair, outfit, and color palette across every view — no drift or warping between views (verified by viewing the image)

the character in reference-sheet.png is on-brief for the spec's CHARACTER_BLOCK (matte black-violet skin, silver-white braided hair, glowing violet eyes, obsidian-and-crimson lamellar armor) on a clean neutral background with no readable text printed in the image (verified by viewing the image)

artifacts/vyre/hero.png exists and is a single clean front-facing portrait of the SAME character as the sheet, on-brief, usable as an i2v start frame — one figure only, no in-image text (verified by viewing the image)

artifacts/vyre/generation-log.md records for EACH asset (reference-sheet.png and hero.png): the producing model (a slug from the pinned chain fal-ai/nano-banana-pro -> openai/gpt-image-2 -> fal-ai/nano-banana-2), the seed 7777, the full composed prompt, any fallbacks taken, and a fal.media URL

the composed prompt logged for each asset reuses the spec's CHARACTER_BLOCK and STYLE_STACK VERBATIM (byte-identical, no paraphrase or synonym substitution) and appends a no-text-in-the-image constraint
the same seed (7777, from the spec) is recorded for both reference-sheet.png and hero.png

evals.prompt: Generate the reference sheet and hero for the project lumen from its character-spec.md, which cites a reference image at inputs/ref.png.
evals.expected_output: Both assets are generated with the reference image passed to the model as --ref, and generation-log.md records that the reference image was used for each asset (path on the ref: line) plus the producing model, seed, full prompt, fallbacks, and fal.media URL.
evals._note: Test setup must materialize artifacts/lumen/inputs/ref.png (a small valid PNG) before this run so the --ref path is exercised. gen-image.sh skips a missing LOCAL ref and degrades to the language+seed lock; without the placeholder, expectations 1-2 (ref passed/logged) are N/A. Create the placeholder at Test wiring time.

files.content:
# Character Spec: Lumen

## Identity Tokens
- face: porcelain-pale freckled face
- hair: copper-red short braid
- eyes: amber eyes
- outfit/props: teal-and-brass aviator jacket

## Seed
4242

## Palette
Copper-amber-teal — warm skin against cool jacket, a single recognizable identity.

## STYLE_STACK
"cinematic concept-art realism, photorealistic render, soft key light, shallow depth of field, sharp focus"

## CHARACTER_BLOCK
"porcelain-pale freckled face, copper-red short braid, amber eyes, teal-and-brass aviator jacket"

## Reference image
inputs/ref.png

## Provenance
brief source: brief + reference image; defaults applied: seed; date: 2026-06-19; model used for the sheet: (recorded by phase 2)

## Downstream use
front-frame: hero.png · identity reference: reference-sheet.png · tokens: CHARACTER_BLOCK

expectations: the reference image inputs/ref.png was passed to the generation as a --ref argument for both reference-sheet.png and hero.png (the spec's Reference image field names a path, not 'none')
artifacts/lumen/generation-log.md records on the ref: line the reference image path (inputs/ref.png) for each asset, not 'none'

artifacts/lumen/reference-sheet.png and artifacts/lumen/hero.png both exist and depict ONE consistent character on-brief for the spec's CHARACTER_BLOCK with no in-image text (verified by viewing the images)
generation-log.md records the producing model, the seed 4242, the full verbatim-block prompt, and a fal.media URL for each asset

evals.prompt: Generate the reference sheet for the project ghost, whose character-spec.md is missing.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that character-spec.md does not exist, records a clean failure in state.md naming the missing required input, and generates no images and no fabricated character.
expectations: no reference-sheet.png or hero.png is generated for artifacts/ghost/ (no character was invented in the absence of the required spec)
the missing required input (character-spec.md) is recorded as a clean failure (state.md is marked blocked and names the missing file) rather than silently skipped or worked around
